questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Venus is placed distant from Sun as compared to Mercury, yet it is the hottest planet.
Reason (R) Immense carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of Venus creates a green house effect.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Ashoka pillars have retained their gloss on their surface.
Reason (R) Moisture laden winds do not blow in the areas where it is located.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Pluto is the coldest planet.
Reason (R) It receives slanty rays of the Sun.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Baking soda creates acidity in the stomach.
Reason (R) Baking soda is alkaline.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Graphite is slippery and used as a lubricant.
Reason (R) Graphite has free electrons.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) There is rainbow in the sky only after rains.
Reason (R) Water drops suspended in the air break up Sun's rays into seven colours.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Mohammad-bin-Tughlaq is called the 'wisest fool'.
Reason (R) He had wise plans but implemented them foolishly.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) DDT has now-a-days lost its use as an insecticide.
Reason (R) DDT is harmful to man.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Buddha left his home soon after his marriage.
Reason (R) He wished to be free of all worldly ties and become an ascetic.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Carbon dioxide turns lime water milky.
Reason (R) Carbon dioxide sullies the water.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Red-green colour blindness occurs with more frequency in males than in females.
Reason (R) Females have two chromosomes and males have one.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Food materials should not be soaked in water for a long time.
Reason (R) Washing leads to loss of vitamin A and vitamin D from the food stuff.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Pressure cookers are fitted with ebonite handles.
Reason (R) Ebonite is a strong conductor of heat.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Earth is the only planet known to have life.
Reason (R) Earth has an atmosphere which is a mixture of oxygen, nitrogen and carbon dioxide.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Leaves of plants are green.
Reason (R) Plants contain chromoplasts, the green pigment.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) The freezing of sea water during winter does not kill the fishes.
Reason (R) Only surface water is frozen.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Moon cannot be used as a satellite for communication.
Reason (R) Moon does not move in the equatorial plane of the Earth.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) There is no vaccine for AIDS.
Reason (R) The AIDS virus changes its genetic code.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) In India, females have higher life expectancy than the males.
Reason (R) Females receive a better diet.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) Unpolished rice should be eaten.
Reason (R) Polished rice lacks vitamin B.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) No food materials normally enters the wind pipe during swallowing.
Reason (R) When we swallow, the back portion of our tongue covers the opening of the wind pipe.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Assertion (A) The western coast of India is characterized by the location of several sea ports.
Reason (R) Western coast has evidence of deep sea water.
